channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Ondo Police Arrest Six Over Suspected Bomb-Making Plot
item.description: Police for Ondo State don stop wetin for don be serious wahala after dem arrest six people wey dem suspect dey plan bomb attack for Akure. According to the State Commissioner of Police, Adebowale Lawa...

item.title: AI Image Misleads About Alleged Rescue of Former Deputy President Gachagua
item.description: A recent image circulating on Facebook claims to show former Kenyan deputy president Rigathi Gachagua being 'saved' from a plot allegedly linked to government officials, specifically interior minister...

item.title: Habal-habal drivers line up at Comelec to secure voter certificates for fuel subsidies
item.description: In Cebu City, hundreds of motorcycle taxi drivers, locally known as habal-habal drivers, have been waiting in long queues outside the Commission on Elections (Comelec) to obtain their voter registrati...

item.title: Explaining the Two Types of UK State Pensions
item.description: The United Kingdom has two types of state pensions, a situation that often causes confusion among retirees. The difference stems from reforms introduced by the government to modernize the pension syst...

item.title: Huawei Introduces the WATCH GT Runner 2 in the Philippines with Enhanced GPS and Durable Titanium Design
item.description: Huawei has officially launched the HUAWEI WATCH GT Runner 2 in the Philippines, targeting runners who seek precision, durability, and comprehensive performance tracking. Priced at Php19,999, pre-order...

item.title: Denis Sassou Nguesso Wins Fifth Term Amid Boycotts and Electoral Concerns in Congo-Brazzaville
item.description: The 2026 presidential election in Congo-Brazzaville saw Denis Sassou Nguesso secure a fifth consecutive term with an official 94.90% of the vote, in what political analysts describe as a predetermined...

item.title: World Athletics rejects Favour Ofili’s request to change nationality to Turkey
item.description: World Athletics has turned down the request by Nigerian sprinter Favour Ofili to switch her sporting allegiance from Nigeria to Turkey. The 24-year-old athlete reportedly sought the change after facin...

item.title: UTME Screening in Progress at Government Secondary School, Apo, Abuja
item.description: Candidates for the 2026 Unified Tertiary Matriculation Examination (UTME) dey undergo screening for di examination for Government Secondary School, Apo Resettlement, Abuja. Di UTME go start from Thurs...

item.title: Calgary faces significant spring snowfall causing travel disruptions
item.description: Calgarians experienced another challenging commute on Thursday as a heavy spring snowstorm brought winter-like conditions back to the city. Environment and Climate Change Canada reported snowfall warn...

item.title: Peter Farquhar Murder Case: Conviction Quashed, Retrial Ordered
item.description: A church warden who was previously sentenced to life imprisonment for the murder of a university lecturer has had his conviction overturned by the Court of Appeal, leading to a re-trial being ordered....

item.title: Mississauga’s Ridgeway Plaza to implement security measures to manage gatherings and traffic
item.description: The City of Mississauga has reached an agreement with the Ridgeway Plaza Condominium Corporations to improve safety and reduce nuisance gatherings at Ridgeway Plaza. The plaza, home to 115 restaurants...

item.title: Labor MP Julie Collins criticised over bulk billing flyer naming GP clinics
item.description: Labor MP Julie Collins has attracted criticism after distributing a flyer across her Franklin electorate in Tasmania to promote Labor’s Bulk Billing Practice Incentive Program (BBPIP). The leaflet hig...

item.title: Supreme Court Sets Guidelines for Functioning and Legal Status of Local Tribunals
item.description: Nairobi — The Supreme Court of Kenya has cleared the air on the role and legal definition of local tribunals following a long-standing dispute involving Parliament. The matter arose from a petition fi...

item.title: Creamline Cool Smashers return to PVL finals after three conferences, eyeing 11th title
item.description: The Creamline Cool Smashers are once again heading to the PVL finals after a three-conference drought, reclaiming their dominant form in Philippine volleyball. The team secured their final berth by de...

item.title: Madlanga Commission of Inquiry Continues Hearing Witness Testimonies
item.description: The Madlanga Commission of Inquiry has resumed proceedings to investigate alleged criminality, political interference, and corruption within South Africa's criminal justice system. On Thursday, the co...

item.title: Retired Army veteran stops chain‑snatching attempt in Hyderabad, felicitated by police
item.description: एक रिटायर्ड आर्मी जवान ने हैदराबाद में एक चेन‑स्नैचिंग की कोशिश को बीच रास्ते में रोक दिया और आरोपी को पकड़वाने में मदद की। घटना शहर के सामान्य इलाके में हुई, जब दो बाइक‑सवार बदमाश एक महिला से सोने की...

item.title: Pre-trial hearing for accused killers of Shailen Singh postponed in Durban High Court
item.description: The family of Shailen Singh, a 32-year-old businessman from Mount Edgecombe, are awaiting justice following the tragic killing of their loved one. Singh, who owned the logistics company Shails Trans, ...

item.title: PCSO Announces No Jackpot Winners in Major Lotto Draws for April 16
item.description: The Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO) reported that no one won the jackpot prizes in the major lotto draws conducted on Thursday, April 16, 2026. The winning numbers for Lotto 6/42, which c...

item.title: Olusegun Mimiko Resigns from PDP, Cites Absence from Party Activities
item.description: Naija, make we yarn you the latest. Former Ondo State Governor, Olusegun Mimiko, don officially resign from the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP). Him send letter dated April 10, 2026, go the chairman of...

item.title: Government to Finalise Public Investment Bill to Strengthen Public Entity Performance
item.description: The Government of Tanzania has announced that it will finalise the Public Investment Bill in the 2026/27 financial year as part of broader reforms to make public institutions more efficient and accoun...
